assertion_results.text: Investigation identifies two independent root causes: (1) convention reference formatter using wrong case transform, (2) task creation using Feature issue type ID instead of Task (Steps 2-4)
assertion_results.evidence: investigation.md Root Cause Summary table lists: Root Cause 1 'Convention reference formatter lowercases and kebab-cases headings instead of preserving original title case' in 'shared/convention-utils.md', and Root Cause 2 'Task creation uses Feature issue type ID (10142) instead of Task issue type ID from config' in 'plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a'. Both are identified in Steps 2-3 with detailed code-path tracing.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard is triggered because the two root causes are in different modules/code paths (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: investigation.md Independence Assessment states: 'Different modules: Root cause 1 is in the shared convention utilities (shared/convention-utils.md), while root cause 2 is in the plan-feature skill logic (plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a)' and 'Different code paths'. Concludes: 'The Decomposition Guard (Step 6) must be triggered because the bug involves multiple independent root causes in different modules requiring separate fixes.' decomposition-guard.md exists confirming the guard was triggered.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard presents both issues with their affected files/modules (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md presents: '1. Convention reference formatter uses wrong case transform (in shared/convention-utils.md)' and '2. Task creation uses wrong issue type ID (in plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a)' with descriptions of each issue.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard offers the user two options: Proceed with single Task or Split into separate Bugs (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md offers: '1. Proceed -- create a single Task covering all fixes' and '2. Split -- I recommend creating separate Bugs for each independent issue, then triaging each one individually' followed by 'Choose (1/2):'.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT silently create a single Task bundling both fixes — it waits for user input (Step 6 stop rule)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md ends with 'Choose (1/2):' and no additional output files exist showing task creation. The outputs directory contains only investigation.md and decomposition-guard.md, confirming the skill stopped at the decomposition guard and waited for user input.
